violations.explanation: Inline page.locator() with POM reference used in test body. The page_additions file defines plan_pause_info as an Elements class attribute. According to the framework pattern, Elements class attributes should be accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.X) ONLY when they are string selectors. However, the correct pattern is to define them as properties in the page object and access directly. This violates Prohibition 6: ZERO page.locator() in test function body.

changes.description: Convert Elements class string selectors to property definitions in PrintHistoryPage

fix_details.explanation: Both changes must be applied: restructure page additions to use property pattern, then update test script to use direct property access

violations.explanation: Same violation as V001 - inline page.locator() wrapping POM reference. Step 5 verification.

violations.current_code: # Step 10: Print simulation uses send_rtp_devicestatus but doesn't specify page count
violations.explanation: Step 10 requires printing exactly 6 pages. The send_rtp_devicestatus call doesn't specify the page count parameter. The method signature needs to be verified to ensure it supports specifying exact page counts.

fix_details.replace_with:
# Send RTP with 6 pages printed
common.send_rtp_devicestatus(
                entity_id=printer_data.entity_id,
                cloud_id=printer_data.cloud_id,
                device_uuid=printer_data.device_uuid,
                pages_printed=6
            )

violations.current_code: # Step 12: Print simulation doesn't specify 9 additional pages
violations.explanation: Step 12 requires printing 9 MORE pages (total 15). The send_rtp_devicestatus call should specify cumulative total of 15 pages or increment by 9.

fix_details.replace_with:
# Send RTP with total 15 pages printed
common.send_rtp_devicestatus(
                entity_id=printer_data.entity_id,
                cloud_id=printer_data.cloud_id,
                device_uuid=printer_data.device_uuid,
                pages_printed=15
            )

violations.expected_code: # Verify PrintHistoryPage has all required locators as properties
violations.explanation: The script instantiates PrintHistoryPage but then accesses elements via .elements. namespace with page.locator() wrapping. The correct pattern per the mapping report is to access locators as direct properties. The page additions file needs to be restructured to define locators as properties in __init__, not as Elements class string attributes.

fix_details.description: Restructure to define locators as properties in PrintHistoryPage.__init__ instead of Elements class strings

violations.current_code:
class Elements:
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    # ... more string selectors
violations.expected_code:
# In PrintHistoryPage.__init__ method:
self.plan_pause_info = page.locator("[data-testid='plan-pause-info']")
self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator("[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']")
# ... more locator properties
violations.explanation: The page additions file defines an Elements class with string selectors. This is NOT the correct pattern for the AURA framework. Per the coding standards and existing page objects in the mapping report, locators should be defined as properties in the page object's __init__ method using page.locator(). The Elements class pattern shown here forces test scripts to use page.locator(page_obj.elements.X) which violates Prohibition 6. The correct pattern is to define self.X = page.locator() in __init__ and access as page_obj.X directly.

fix_details.description: Convert Elements class string selectors to property definitions in PrintHistoryPage.__init__
fix_details.current_structure:
class Elements:
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    complimentary_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-progress-bar']"
    complimentary_pages_value = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-value']"
    complimentary_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon']"
    complimentary_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-message']"
    complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-tooltip']"
    additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
    additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='additional-pages-tooltip']"
fix_details.required_structure:
# Add to PrintHistoryPage.__init__ method:
self.plan_pause_info = page.locator("[data-testid='plan-pause-info']")
self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator("[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']")
self.complimentary_pages_progress_bar = page.locator("[data-testid='complimentary-pages-progress-bar']")
self.complimentary_pages_value = page.locator("[data-testid='complimentary-pages-value']")
self.complimentary_pages_info_icon = page.locator("[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon']")
self.complimentary_pages_info_message = page.locator("[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-message']")
self.complimentary_pages_tooltip = page.locator("[data-testid='complimentary-pages-tooltip']")
self.additional_pages_progress_bar = page.locator("[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']")
self.additional_pages_value = page.locator("[data-testid='additional-pages-value']")
self.additional_pages_info_icon = page.locator("[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']")
self.additional_pages_info_message = page.locator("[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']")
self.additional_pages_tooltip = page.locator("[data-testid='additional-pages-tooltip']")

violations.expected_code: # Update with actual tooltip selectors when available
violations.explanation: The page additions file defines tooltip locators with TODO comments indicating these selectors may not be accurate. The comment states 'TODO: Update with specific data-testid when available'. These locators are referenced in the test script but may not work correctly if the actual tooltip elements use different selectors (e.g., [role='tooltip'] or dynamic IDs). This creates a risk of test failures.

fix_details.description: Verify tooltip selectors match actual DOM elements or update with correct selectors

self.additional_pages_tooltip = page.locator("[data-testid='additional-pages-tooltip']")  # TODO: Verify selector

violations.expected_code:

def verify_plan_pause_info_not_displayed(self):

violations.explanation: The page additions file defines methods outside of any class context. These methods should be added to the PrintHistoryPage class body. Additionally, the methods reference self.elements.plan_pause_info which is incorrect - they should reference self.plan_pause_info directly after the Elements class is restructured to property definitions.
